messages.content: "Welcome home" in Japanese can be translated as "おかえり" (okaeri) when speaking informally, typically to someone with whom you have a close relationship, like a family member. If you wish to be more polite, you could say "おかえりなさい" (okaeri nasai).

Translate to Japanese
"Footage of Two Invisible Men"
"Footage of Two Invisible Men" in Japanese would be "二人の透明人間の映像" (Futari no tōmei ningen no eizō).

Translate to Japanese
"Front Role Seats at NASA"

Literally, front row seats to a NASA rocket launch.
"Front row seats to a NASA rocket launch" translated to Japanese would be "NASAのロケット発射の最前列席" (NASA no roketto hassha no saizenretsu seki).
"Beautiful sing-song rhymes" in Japanese can be translated as "美しい歌うような韻を踏んだ詩" (Utsukushii utau yōna in o funda shi).

Translate to Japanese
"A new kind of gal"
"A new kind of gal" translates to Japanese as "新しいタイプの女の子" (Atarashii taipu no onna no ko).

"That guy" can be translated to Japanese as "あの人" (ano hito) if referring to someone in a neutral or polite manner, or "あいつ" (aitsu) if referring to someone casually or with a somewhat derogatory tone.

Translate to Japanese
"The Missing Eye of Jesus Christ"
"The Missing Eye of Jesus Christ" in Japanese could be translated as "イエス・キリストの失われた目" (Iesu Kirisuto no ushinawareta me).

Translate to Japanese
"Tears in the Rain"
"Tears in the Rain" in Japanese is "雨の中の涙" (Ame no naka no namida).

Translate to Korean
"Analogue: A Hate Story"
"Analogue: A Hate Story" translated to Korean is "아날로그: 미움 이야기" (Analogue: Mium Iyagi).
